content_markdown:
A wave pulse is sent along a light string which is attached to a heavy rope as shown. The diagrams are not to scale.

Which diagram shows the shape of the string and the rope after a short time?

diagram_metadata.description: A diagram showing a positive displacement wave pulse traveling to the right on a thin 'light string' towards a junction with a thicker 'heavy rope'.

options.label: A
options.text: A diagram showing a reflected pulse on the light string with positive displacement moving left, and a transmitted pulse on the heavy rope with positive displacement moving right.

options.label: B
options.text: A diagram showing a reflected pulse on the light string with negative displacement (inverted) moving left, and a transmitted pulse on the heavy rope with positive displacement moving right.

options.label: C
options.text: A diagram showing a reflected pulse on the light string with positive displacement moving left, and a transmitted pulse on the heavy rope with negative displacement (inverted) moving right.

options.label: D
options.text: A diagram showing a reflected pulse on the light string with negative displacement (inverted) moving left, and a transmitted pulse on the heavy rope with negative displacement (inverted) moving right.

vibe_explanation: When a wave pulse travels from a less dense medium (light string) to a more dense medium (heavy rope), the boundary acts somewhat like a fixed end. This results in a reflected pulse that is phase-shifted by 180 degrees (inverted). The transmitted pulse, however, never undergoes a phase change and remains upright (positive displacement).
